content_markdown:
The diagram shows an electric circuit containing a potentiometer of maximum resistance $R$. The potentiometer is connected in series with a resistor also of resistance $R$. The electromotive force (emf) of the battery is $6\text{ V}$ and its internal resistance is negligible.

The slider on the potentiometer is moved from $P_1$ to $P_2$. Which graph shows the variation of the voltmeter $V$ reading with slider distance $d$?

diagram_metadata.description: A circuit diagram with a 6V battery, a fixed resistor R, and a potentiometer R in series. A voltmeter is connected across the portion of the potentiometer between P1 and the slider.

options.label: A
options.text: A graph showing V starting at 0 at P1 and increasing linearly to 6V at P2.

options.label: B
options.text: A graph showing V starting at a non-zero value at P1 and increasing linearly to 6V at P2.

options.label: C
options.text: A graph showing V starting at 0 at P1 and increasing linearly to 3V at P2.

options.label: D
options.text: A graph showing V starting at 6V at P1 and decreasing linearly towards P2.

vibe_explanation: The total resistance in the circuit is $R + R = 2R$. Since the battery is $6\text{ V}$, the potential difference across the entire potentiometer (from $P_1$ to $P_2$) is $3\text{ V}$ by the potential divider rule ($V_{pot} = 6 \times \frac{R}{R+R}$). At $P_1$, the voltmeter is shorted ($d=0$), so $V = 0$. At $P_2$, the voltmeter measures the full potential across the potentiometer, which is $3\text{ V}$. The relationship is linear with distance $d$.
